title: Workload definition (Contract 3)
description: A reproducible load-generation workload for inferbench. Normative. Every field that shapes traffic is explicit and required — there are no implicit defaults, because the benchmark comparability rule (compatibility-policy.md §7) keys on workload version + seed. The eight named workloads under examples/workloads/ are NON-NORMATIVE fixtures; the canonical versioned suite is owned by inferbench (IB-T003).

version.description: SemVer of this workload definition. Any change to any traffic-shaping field bumps it; results are comparable only across identical workload version + seed.

seed.description: PRNG seed. All sampling (arrival times, lengths, cancellation points, slow-client assignment) MUST derive deterministically from this seed.

description.description: Human-readable intent of the workload (explanatory, not traffic-shaping).

arrival_process.description: Exactly one of open-loop Poisson (rate-driven; queueing delay visible) or closed-loop (concurrency-driven; MUST carry the disclosure flag because closed-loop arrival hides queueing delay under saturation).

rate_rps.description: Mean arrival rate (requests/second) for a single-phase workload. Mutually exclusive with phases.

phases.description: Piecewise-constant rate schedule for time-varying (e.g. bursty) workloads. Mutually exclusive with rate_rps.

repeat_phases.description: If true, the phase schedule repeats cyclically until the stop condition; if false or absent, arrivals stop when the schedule is exhausted (even if the stop condition has not fired).

concurrency.description: Number of concurrent client loops; each issues its next request only after the previous one completes (plus think time).

think_time_seconds.description: Fixed delay each loop waits between completing a request and sending the next. Absent means 0.

closed_loop_disclosed.description: MANDATORY disclosure flag, literally true. Closed-loop arrival throttles offered load to service capacity, hiding queueing delay and understating tail latency under saturation (coordinated-omission-adjacent bias). Every report of a closed-loop run MUST label it as closed-loop; a closed-loop workload without this flag is invalid.

input_length_distribution.description: Distribution of input (prompt) length in tokens, measured with the tokenizer recorded in the run manifest. Sampled values are rounded to the nearest integer >= 1.

output_length_distribution.description: Distribution of requested output length in tokens (drives max_tokens per request). Sampled values are rounded to the nearest integer >= 1.

ratio.description: Fraction of requests whose prompt begins with a shared prefix. 0 = no sharing (still explicitly declared).

shared_prefix_length_tokens.description: Length of the shared prefix in tokens. Required when ratio > 0.

group_size.description: Number of distinct requests sharing each prefix group before a new prefix is generated. Absent means one global shared prefix.

cancellation.description: Client-initiated cancellation profile. rate 0 = no cancellations (still explicitly declared). When rate > 0, the cancellation point MUST be specified.

rate.description: Fraction of requests the client cancels (connection close per Contract 1 cancellation semantics).

trigger.description: Whether the cancellation point is sampled in wall-clock seconds since send, or in output tokens received.

distribution.description: Distribution of the cancellation point, in the trigger's unit. Token-valued samples are rounded to the nearest integer >= 0.

slow_client.description: Slow-client profile: a fraction of clients read the response stream slower than the server produces it (exercises gateway write-buffer bounds and write deadlines, fault scenario 4). fraction 0 = no slow clients (still explicitly declared).

fraction.description: Fraction of requests issued by throttled-read clients.

read_bytes_per_second.description: Sustained client read throughput applied to the response stream. Required when fraction > 0.

initial_read_delay_seconds.description: Optional delay before the slow client starts reading the response at all.

stop.description: Exactly one stop condition: wall-clock duration or total request count.

tags.description: Free-form labels for report grouping (explanatory, not traffic-shaping).

distribution.description: A 1-D sampling distribution. Values are numbers; token-valued uses round to integers as stated at the point of use.

samples.description: Sampled uniformly (with replacement) from this list.

weight.description: Relative weight; weights are normalized to sum to 1.
